acknowledged.description: For a successful response, this value is always true. On failure, an exception is returned instead.

_types__Duration.description:
A duration. Units can be `nanos`, `micros`, `ms` (milliseconds), `s` (seconds), `m` (minutes), `h` (hours) and
`d` (days). Also accepts "0" without a unit and "-1" to indicate an unspecified value.

_types__DateTime.description:
A date and time, either as a string whose format can depend on the context (defaulting to ISO 8601), or a
number of milliseconds since the Epoch. Elasticsearch accepts both as input, but will generally output a string
representation.

failed.description: The number of shards the operation or search attempted to run on but failed.

successful.description: The number of shards the operation or search succeeded on.

total.description: The number of shards the operation or search will run on overall.

_types__MinimumShouldMatch.description: The minimum number of terms that should match as integer, percentage or range

params.description:
Specifies any named parameters that are passed into the script as variables.
Use parameters instead of hard-coded values to decrease compile time.

lang.description: Specifies the language the script is written in.

oneOf.description: Path to field or array of paths. Some API's support wildcards in the path to select multiple fields.

items.description: Path to field or array of paths. Some API's support wildcards in the path to select multiple fields.

oneOf.description: Path to field or array of paths. Some API's support wildcards in the path to select multiple fields.

boost.description:
Floating point number used to decrease or increase the relevance scores of the query.
Boost values are relative to the default value of 1.0.
A boost value between 0 and 1.0 decreases the relevance score.
A value greater than 1.0 increases the relevance score.

field.description: The name of the vector field to search against

query_vector_builder.description: The query vector builder. You must provide a query_vector_builder or query_vector, but not both.

num_candidates.description: The number of nearest neighbor candidates to consider per shard

visit_percentage.description: The percentage of vectors to explore per shard while doing knn search with bbq_disk

k.description: The final number of nearest neighbors to return as top hits

filter.description: Filters for the kNN search query

similarity.description: The minimum similarity for a vector to be considered a match

rescore_vector.description: Apply oversampling and rescoring to quantized vectors

lookup.description:
Lookup a vector from an existing document.
Must reference a dense_vector field and a single value.

_types__KnnEmbeddingInput.description:
Knn embedding input.
Either a string, an object or array of objects

type.description: The type of data that the value represents.

format.description: The format of the data. If null, the default data format for the given type is used.

value.description: String which may be raw text, or the string representation of some other data such as an image in base64.

model_id.description:
Model ID is required for all dense_vector fields but
may be inferred for semantic_text fields

model_text.description: The text to be converted into a vector by the specified model

id.description: The ID of the document to fetch the vector from

index.description: The name of the index to fetch the document from

path.description: The name of the field containing the vector

routing.description: The routing value to use when fetching the document

oversample.description: Applies the specified oversample factor to k on the approximate kNN search

_types__Routing.description: Only to be used in query and path parameters, as the array form is actually a csv

tokens_freq_ratio_threshold.description: Tokens whose frequency is more than this threshold times the average frequency of all tokens in the specified field are considered outliers and pruned.

tokens_weight_threshold.description: Tokens whose weight is less than this threshold are considered nonsignificant and pruned.

only_score_pruned_tokens.description: Whether to only score pruned tokens, vs only scoring kept tokens.

_types__GeoBounds.description:
A geo bounding box. It can be represented in various ways:
- as 4 top/bottom/left/right coordinates
- as 2 top_left / bottom_right points
- as 2 top_right / bottom_left points
- as a WKT bounding box

_types__GeoLocation.description:
A latitude/longitude as a 2 dimensional point. It can be represented in various ways:
- as a `{lat, long}` object
- as a geo hash value
- as a `[lon, lat]` array
- as a string in `"<lat>, <lon>"` or WKT point formats

_types__GeoHashPrecision.description: A precision that can be expressed as a geohash length between 1 and 12, or a distance measure like "1km", "10m".

field.description: The name of the vector field to search against

query_vector_builder.description: The query vector builder. You must provide a query_vector_builder or query_vector, but not both.

k.description: The final number of nearest neighbors to return as top hits

num_candidates.description: The number of nearest neighbor candidates to consider per shard

visit_percentage.description: The percentage of vectors to explore per shard while doing knn search with bbq_disk

boost.description: Boost value to apply to kNN scores

filter.description: Filters for the kNN search query

similarity.description: The minimum similarity for a vector to be considered a match

inner_hits.description: If defined, each search hit will contain inner hits.

rescore_vector.description: Apply oversampling and rescoring to quantized vectors

standard.description: A retriever that replaces the functionality of a traditional query.

knn.description: A retriever that replaces the functionality  of a knn search.

rrf.description: A retriever that produces top documents from reciprocal rank fusion (RRF).

text_similarity_reranker.description: A retriever that reranks the top documents based on a reranking model using the InferenceAPI

rule.description: A retriever that replaces the functionality of a rule query.

rescorer.description: A retriever that re-scores only the results produced by its child retriever.

linear.description: A retriever that supports the combination of different retrievers through a weighted linear combination.

pinned.description:
A pinned retriever applies pinned documents to the underlying retriever.
This retriever will rewrite to a PinnedQueryBuilder.

diversify.description: A retriever that diversifies the results from its child retriever.

filter.description: Query to filter the documents that can match.

min_score.description: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in the top documents.

query.description: Defines a query to retrieve a set of top documents.

search_after.description: Defines a search after object parameter used for pagination.

terminate_after.description: Maximum number of documents to collect for each shard.

sort.description: A sort object that that specifies the order of matching documents.

collapse.description: Collapses the top documents by a specified key into a single top document per key.

filter.description: Query to filter the documents that can match.

min_score.description: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in the top documents.

field.description: The name of the vector field to search against.

query_vector.description: Query vector. Must have the same number of dimensions as the vector field you are searching against. You must provide a query_vector_builder or query_vector, but not both.

query_vector_builder.description: Defines a model to build a query vector.

k.description: Number of nearest neighbors to return as top hits.

num_candidates.description: Number of nearest neighbor candidates to consider per shard.

visit_percentage.description: The percentage of vectors to explore per shard while doing knn search with bbq_disk

similarity.description: The minimum similarity required for a document to be considered a match.

rescore_vector.description: Apply oversampling and rescoring to quantized vectors

filter.description: Query to filter the documents that can match.

min_score.description: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in the top documents.

retrievers.description: A list of child retrievers to specify which sets of returned top documents will have the RRF formula applied to them. Each retriever can optionally include a weight parameter.

rank_constant.description: This value determines how much influence documents in individual result sets per query have over the final ranked result set.

rank_window_size.description: This value determines the size of the individual result sets per query.

_types__RRFRetrieverEntry.description: Either a direct RetrieverContainer (backward compatible) or an RRFRetrieverComponent with weight.

weight.description: Weight multiplier for this retriever's contribution to the RRF score. Higher values increase influence. Defaults to 1.0 if not specified. Must be non-negative.

_types__RRFRetrieverComponent.description: Wraps a retriever with an optional weight for RRF scoring.

filter.description: Query to filter the documents that can match.

min_score.description: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in the top documents.

retriever.description: The nested retriever which will produce the first-level results, that will later be used for reranking.

rank_window_size.description: This value determines how many documents we will consider from the nested retriever.

inference_id.description: Unique identifier of the inference endpoint created using the inference API.

inference_text.description: The text snippet used as the basis for similarity comparison.

field.description: The document field to be used for text similarity comparisons. This field should contain the text that will be evaluated against the inference_text.

chunk_rescorer.description: Whether to rescore on only the best matching chunks.

size.description: The number of chunks per document to evaluate for reranking.

filter.description: Query to filter the documents that can match.

min_score.description: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in the top documents.

ruleset_ids.description: The ruleset IDs containing the rules this retriever is evaluating against.

match_criteria.description: The match criteria that will determine if a rule in the provided rulesets should be applied.

retriever.description: The retriever whose results rules should be applied to.

rank_window_size.description: This value determines the size of the individual result set.

filter.description: Query to filter the documents that can match.

min_score.description: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in the top documents.

filter.description: Query to filter the documents that can match.

min_score.description: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in the top documents.

filter.description: Query to filter the documents that can match.

min_score.description: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in the top documents.

filter.description: Query to filter the documents that can match.

min_score.description: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in the top documents.

field.description: The document field on which to diversify results on.

retriever.description: The nested retriever whose results will be diversified.

size.description: The number of top documents to return after diversification.

rank_window_size.description: The number of top documents from the nested retriever to consider for diversification.

query_vector.description: The query vector used for diversification.

query_vector_builder.description: a dense vector query vector builder to use instead of a static query_vector

lambda.description: Controls the trade-off between relevance and diversity for MMR. A value of 0.0 focuses solely on diversity, while a value of 1.0 focuses solely on relevance. Required for MMR

field.description: Path to field or array of paths. Some API's support wildcards in the path to select multiple fields.

batches.description: The number of scroll responses pulled back by the reindex.

created.description: The number of documents that were successfully created.

deleted.description: The number of documents that were successfully deleted.

noops.description: The number of documents that were ignored because the script used for the reindex returned a `noop` value for `ctx.op`.

requests_per_second.description: The number of requests per second effectively executed during the reindex.

retries.description: The number of retries attempted by reindex. `bulk` is the number of bulk actions retried and `search` is the number of search actions retried.

throttled_millis.description: Number of milliseconds the request slept to conform to `requests_per_second`.

throttled_until_millis.description:
This field should always be equal to zero in a `_reindex` response.
It only has meaning when using the Task API, where it indicates the next time (in milliseconds since epoch) a throttled request will be executed again in order to conform to `requests_per_second`.

total.description: The number of documents that were successfully processed.

updated.description: The number of documents that were successfully updated, for example, a document with same ID already existed prior to reindex updating it.

version_conflicts.description: The number of version conflicts that reindex hits.

cancelled.description: The reason for cancellation if the slice was canceled

bulk.description: The number of bulk actions retried.

search.description: The number of search actions retried.

batches.description: The number of scroll responses pulled back by the reindex.

created.description: The number of documents that were successfully created.

deleted.description: The number of documents that were successfully deleted.

failures.description: Any failures encountered during the reindex. If non-empty, the reindex ended because of these failures.

noops.description: The number of documents that were ignored because the script returned a `noop` value for `ctx.op`.

requests_per_second.description: The number of requests per second effectively executed during the reindex.

retries.description: The number of retries attempted by reindex.

throttled_millis.description: Number of milliseconds the request slept to conform to `requests_per_second`.

throttled_until_millis.description: This field should always be equal to zero in a completed reindex result.

timed_out.description: Whether any of the requests executed during the reindex timed out.

took.description: The total milliseconds the entire operation took.

total.description: The number of documents that were successfully processed.

updated.description: The number of documents that were successfully updated.

version_conflicts.description: The number of version conflicts that occurred.

_types__ReindexTaskResult.description:
The final result of a completed reindex operation, as stored in the task result.
This is the serialized form of `BulkByScrollResponse`.

_id.description: The unique identifier for the added document.

_index.description: The name of the index the document was added to.

_primary_term.description: The primary term assigned to the document for the indexing operation.

result.description: The result of the indexing operation: `created` or `updated`.

_seq_no.description:
The sequence number assigned to the document for the indexing operation.
Sequence numbers are used to ensure an older version of a document doesn't overwrite a newer version.

_shards.description: Information about the replication process of the operation.

_version.description: The document version, which is incremented each time the document is updated.

failure_store.description: The role of the failure store in this document response

_types__Slices.description: Slices configuration used to parallelize a process.

lang.description:
The language the script is written in.
For search templates, use `mustache`.

source.description:
The script source.
For search templates, an object containing the search template.

acknowledged.description: For a successful response, this value is always true. On failure, an exception is returned instead.

build_hash.description: The Elasticsearch Git commit's SHA hash.

build_snapshot.description: Indicates whether the Elasticsearch build was a snapshot.

build_type.description:
The build type that corresponds to how Elasticsearch was installed.
For example, `docker`, `rpm`, or `tar`.

lucene_version.description: The version number of Elasticsearch's underlying Lucene software.

minimum_index_compatibility_version.description: The minimum index version with which the responding node can read from disk.

minimum_wire_compatibility_version.description:
The minimum node version with which the responding node can communicate.
Also the minimum version from which you can perform a rolling upgrade.

number.description:
The Elasticsearch version number.

::: IMPORTANT: For Serverless deployments, this static value is always `8.11.0` and is used solely for backward compatibility with legacy clients.
 Serverless environments are versionless and automatically upgraded, so this value can be safely ignored.

id.description: The ID of the reindex task. The ID is assigned when the task was first created and remains the same across graceful shutdown relocations.

description.description: A sanitized description of the reindex operation (source and destination indices, and optionally remote host info).

start_time_in_millis.description: The time at which the reindex task started, in milliseconds since the Unix epoch. Remains the same across graceful shutdown relocations.

start_time.description:
The time at which the reindex task started, as an ISO-8601 formatted string. Remains the same across graceful shutdown relocations.
Only present when the request includes the `?human=true` query parameter.

running_time.description:
The elapsed running time of the reindex task, including relocations, in a human-readable format.
Only present when the request includes the `?human=true` query parameter.

running_time_in_nanos.description: The elapsed running time of the reindex task, including relocations, in nanoseconds.

cancelled.description: Whether the reindex task has been cancelled.

status.description: The current progress of the reindex operation.

_types__ReindexTaskInfo.description: Information about a single parent reindex task, as returned by the reindex management APIs.

allow_no_indices.description:
A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression.
If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression
(including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the
complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all
expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no
indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

expand_wildcards.description:
Type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument
determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams. Supports comma-separated values,
such as `open,hidden`.

ignore_unavailable.description:
If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded)
index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable.
If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

ignore_throttled.description: If true, concrete, expanded or aliased indices are ignored when frozen.

_types__IndicesOptions.description:
Controls how to deal with unavailable concrete indices (closed or missing), how wildcard expressions are expanded
to actual indices (all, closed or open indices) and how to deal with wildcard expressions that resolve to no indices.

_types__ErrorResponseBase.description: The response returned by Elasticsearch when request execution did not succeed.
